# Gazelle Azure Platform

Gazelle is an internal Azure landing zone platform. It provisions isolated Azure environments for application teams — each with its own billing, identity, network, and guardrails — fully managed through GitHub. No portal clicks, no tickets. Teams get a subscription, a repo, and a pipeline; the platform enforces the rules.

## Design Principles

Deny by default: Every Azure resource type is blocked until the platform has wired security controls, diagnostic settings, and access roles for it.

Self-service landing zones: Application teams own their environments end-to-end. The platform provisions guardrails; teams control what they build and how they run it.

No human touch: Configuration lives in the repo. Every change flows through a branch, a PR, and a pipeline. Nothing is set manually in the Azure portal or GitHub UI.

Zero cost on idle: Platform-managed infrastructure carries no flat fees. Every service scales to zero when unused.

## Azure Services

Management Groups — Organise subscriptions into a governance hierarchy (prod, test, subscription-bank). Policy, RBAC, and budgets are scoped here.

Azure Policy — Deny-by-default resource controls, diagnostic settings deployment, tag enforcement, and security baselines. Custom definitions close gaps where built-ins only audit.

Deployment Stacks — Declarative resource lifecycle. What's in the code is what exists in Azure — removed resources are deleted automatically on next deployment.

Managed Identities — One user-assigned identity per landing zone, federated with GitHub OIDC. Used by workflows, automation jobs, and Entra ID queries.

Entra ID — App registrations for platform identity, groups for RBAC, federated credentials for passwordless auth from GitHub Actions.

Virtual Networks — Isolated VNet per landing zone. No peering, no on-prem connectivity. Includes a delegated subnet for GitHub-hosted runners.

Log Analytics — One workspace per landing zone. Diagnostic settings are deployed automatically via policy for every whitelisted resource type.

Container Apps Jobs — Scheduled automation (tag remediation, role cleanup, policy remediation) running inside each landing zone subscription. Images pulled from GitHub Container Registry.

Budgets — Monthly cost ceiling per subscription with forecasted-spend alerts routed to the landing zone's action group.

Action Groups — Email notifications scoped to the landing zone's engineers. Used by budget alerts and activity log alerts.

Defender for Cloud — Security contact configuration per landing zone. Paid-SKU recommendations are exempted by the platform.

Custom RBAC Roles — Job-function-scoped roles (e.g. App Engineer) instead of per-resource-type roles.

## GitHub Services

GitHub Actions — CI/CD for both platform and landing zone deployments. Reusable workflows enforce the test-then-prod promotion gate.

GitHub-hosted Runners (VNet-injected) — Private runners inside the landing zone VNet, providing network access to PaaS data planes that deny public access.

GitHub Container Registry — Shared image source for automation jobs. Avoids Azure Container Registry's fixed monthly cost.

GitHub Apps — Platform identity for repo creation, variable seeding, and runner registration.

## Important

This file is a summary. Before answering any question about Gazelle, read the full knowledge graph at https://gazelle.cloud/llm-full.txt
